_doc.tiers: Each provider has three tier slots: flagship (highest quality), standard (default), fast (cheapest/quickest). These map 1:1 to Claude Code's internal slots — flagship→opus, standard→sonnet, fast→haiku — used by `/model opus|sonnet|haiku` inside a session. The catalog uses semantic names; core.ps1/core.sh translates to Claude Code's env var contract.
_doc.contextByTier: Per-tier max context (tokens) when tiers differ. Picker shows min-max range. Omit when uniform.
_doc.qualityTier: Sort/tag label: flagship | standard | budget | free | slow.
_doc.envVars: Extra ANTHROPIC_* or CLAUDE_CODE_* env vars to set during launch. Applied AFTER the auto-context block so a provider can override the auto-derived values.
_doc._auto_context: core.ps1/core.sh auto-sets CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS + DISABLE_COMPACT=1 whenever the flagship tier context is >=500K. Threshold cleanly separates 1M-class providers (deepseek, mimo, qwen, xiaomi v2.5-pro) from 256K models (kimi) and 200K models (minimax/glm) where losing auto-compact would be a bad trade. Source: contextByTier.flagship if present, else context. Tradeoff: when flagship/standard are large but fast is small (e.g. mimo flagship 1M / fast 256K), the env var stays at the flagship size for the whole session — /model haiku will display the larger value but the API will reject prompts beyond the fast model's real ceiling. Acceptable for opus-primary workflows. To opt a sub-500K provider in, set envVars explicitly in its block.
_doc._note_nvidia: NVIDIA NIM model IDs change frequently — verify at https://build.nvidia.com/explore/discover before relying on a tier mapping.
_doc._tier_mapping: flagship → /model opus, standard → /model sonnet, fast → /model haiku

deepseek.notes: Direct API via Anthropic-native /anthropic endpoint. Only V4 Pro and V4 Flash exist — Standard tier maps to Pro (no middle SKU). All tiers are 1M context. core.ps1/core.sh auto-derives CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1000000 + DISABLE_COMPACT=1 from the 1M context (see _doc._auto_context). Tradeoff: no auto-compaction — use /compact manually near the limit.

gemini.notes: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro (preview) via OpenRouter, 1M context, strong software-engineering coder. Single SKU on all three slots. 1M crosses the 500K threshold so core.ps1/core.sh auto-derive CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1000000 + DISABLE_COMPACT=1.

glm.notes: GLM-5.2 SOTA open-weight, MIT licensed, 1M context (released 2026-06-13). Fast tier is GLM-4.7-Flash — no 5.2-branded air SKU exists. 1M crosses the 500K threshold so core.ps1/core.sh auto-derive CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1000000 + DISABLE_COMPACT=1 (use /compact manually near the limit). Use cc-glm instead of cc-zai-glm51 (China endpoint is slow).

grok.notes: xAI Grok 4.20 via OpenRouter — 2M context (largest in the catalog), reasoning model. Single SKU on all three slots. core.ps1/core.sh auto-derive CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=2000000 + DISABLE_COMPACT=1 from the 2M context.

kimi.notes: 1T params / 32B active MoE. Context is 256K, NOT 1M — Kimi K2.7 ships only as 'K2.7 Code' and did not bump the window from K2.6. Single-tier (same model on all three slots), so it stays below the 500K auto-context threshold by design.

minimax.notes: Direct API. MiniMax M3 is 1M context (released 2026-05-31). IMPORTANT: the /anthropic endpoint self-reports only ~200K in its model metadata (a known upstream bug inheriting Claude Sonnet's value), which makes Claude Code auto-compact early (~167K) unless overridden — so context is set to 1000000 here on purpose to drive CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1000000 + DISABLE_COMPACT=1 (see _doc._auto_context). No verified M3-highspeed fast SKU, so all three tiers map to MiniMax-M3.

minimax-or.notes: MiniMax M3 via OpenRouter — 1M context, US/EU-latency alternative to cc-minimax (direct api.minimax.io). Unlike the direct endpoint, OpenRouter reports the real 1M window, so no self-report workaround needed. Single SKU on all three slots.

mimo.notes: Tier contexts differ: flagship/standard are 1M (1024K), V2-Flash (fast tier) is 256K. The 'context' field shows the safe minimum across tiers. Verified 2026-05-03 via OpenRouter pricing API. core.ps1/core.sh auto-derives CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1048576 from contextByTier.flagship (see _doc._auto_context). Caveat: env var stays at 1M for the whole session, so /model haiku (v2-flash, 256K) will display 1M but the API will reject prompts beyond ~256K — acceptable for opus-primary workflows.

nemotron.notes: FREE via OpenRouter. 120B hybrid MoE (Mamba+Transformer), 12B active. 50%+ faster than transformer-only models. Multi-agent applications, cross-document reasoning. cc-nvidia (direct API) is separate — this is the OpenRouter-hosted free tier.

nvidia.notes: Free tier with rate limits. Tier IDs are best-guess defaults — verify at https://build.nvidia.com/explore/discover and edit this JSON to taste. cc-nvidia <model-id> overrides all three tiers.

ollama-glm.notes: GLM-5.2 on Ollama Cloud (hosted inference). Ollama speaks the Anthropic Messages API at https://ollama.com; auth via OLLAMA_API_KEY (key from ollama.com/settings/keys, sent as ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN). The model tag is exactly glm-5.2:cloud — the :cloud suffix is mandatory (it routes to hosted inference instead of a local pull). Context set to the 976K window Ollama lists for this tag (not the full 1M) so CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS doesn't over-promise. Smoke-test once: Ollama's docs mostly show the localhost:11434 path, the direct cloud Anthropic base is community/issue-attested.

ollama-minimax.notes: MiniMax M3 on Ollama Cloud (hosted inference). Anthropic Messages API at https://ollama.com; auth via OLLAMA_API_KEY (sent as ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN). The model tag is exactly minimax-m3:cloud — the :cloud suffix is mandatory. Ollama serves this tag at a 512K window (the model's guaranteed minimum), so context is set to 512K here, NOT the full 1M — for the guaranteed 1M MiniMax M3 use cc-minimax (direct) or cc-minimax-or (OpenRouter). Smoke-test the cloud Anthropic base once before relying on it.

qwen.notes: Tier contexts differ: only Qwen3.7-Max (flagship) is 1M; Qwen3-Coder and Qwen3-Coder-Next are 256K each. The 'context' field shows the safe minimum across tiers. Qwen3.7-Max is the current flagship (released 2026-05-21, supersedes qwen3.6-plus). core.ps1/core.sh auto-derives CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1000000 from contextByTier.flagship (see _doc._auto_context). Caveat: /model sonnet|haiku (256K coders) will display 1M but the API will reject prompts beyond 256K — acceptable for opus-primary workflows.

xiaomi.notes: Direct Xiaomi MiMo token-plan endpoint (Singapore region). Anthropic-native, distinct from cc-mimo (OpenRouter). Tier IDs verified 2026-05-09 against /v1/models listing — gateway exposes only mimo-v2.5-pro, mimo-v2.5, and mimo-v2-pro for chat (plus omni/TTS variants not used here). No 'flash' SKU exists on this gateway, so the fast slot maps to the older-gen mimo-v2-pro. Auth uses x-api-key header in Anthropic format. Flagship (mimo-v2.5-pro) is 1M context — confirmed by Ryan 2026-05-09. Standard/fast contexts on this direct gateway are not yet verified, so kept conservative at the 256K floor. core.ps1/core.sh auto-derives CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=1048576 from contextByTier.flagship.

codex.notes: Uses OAuth token cached at ~/.config/codex-oauth/token.json. Run cc-codex-login first.

opencode-minimax.displayName: MiniMax M3 via OpenCode Go (US endpoint)
opencode-minimax.notes: US-hosted MiniMax M3 via OpenCode Go subscription. Anthropic-format compatible (MiniMax/Qwen route to the Anthropic surface on Go; GLM/Kimi do NOT — they're OpenAI-only there). M3 is natively 1M but OpenCode Go's effective cap is unverified, so context kept conservative at ~200K. For guaranteed 1M MiniMax M3 use cc-minimax (direct) or cc-minimax-or (OpenRouter).

owl-alpha.notes: FREE cloaked alpha model, 1M context, agentic-optimized. Tool calling verified working via Anthropic Messages API (2026-05-26). Stealth provider — prompts/completions may be logged. Intermittent at peak times; retry if first launch errors. Best for exploratory sessions.

zai-glm51.notes: China-based endpoint. GLM-5.2 with the full 1M window — the [1m] suffix is required on this endpoint (bare glm-5.2 gives the default smaller window). Fast/haiku companion is glm-4.7 (replaces glm-4.5-air). Command is still cc-zai-glm51 for backward compatibility. Use cc-glm (OpenRouter) instead for the same model with US/EU latency.
